title: Avoid Boxing Primitive Values for String Conversion
description: Boxing is the process of putting a primitive value into a primitive-wrapper object. This practice should be avoided when done purely to use the wrapper class' toString method, as it is a waste of memory and cycles because those methods are static.
recommendation: Use static methods like Integer.toString(myInt) instead of creating an Integer object.
impact: Improves performance by reducing memory and CPU usage.

checks.message: Use static methods like Integer.toString(myInt) instead of creating an Integer object.
